insights:
The single best deal among current models is MiMo-V2.5, which delivers the highest value score of 816.7. It achieves a strong intelligence benchmark of 49 out of 60 while costing only $0.0600 per million tokens, matching the lowest blended price in the top tier. Its 93 tokens per second throughput is solid, and it supports a 1000K context window, making it suitable for long-document tasks. Arena ELO ratings of 1434 overall and 1440 for code further confirm its practical performance. For developers prioritizing the best balance of capability and cost, MiMo-V2.5 is the clear leader.

Notable runners-up include DeepSeek V4 Flash, which also costs $0.0600 per million tokens and achieves a value score of 783.3 with an intelligence score of 47. It offers faster generation at 128 tokens per second and the same 1000K context, making it a close alternative if speed is more important than a marginal intelligence gain. MiMo-V2-Flash, at the same price point, provides 128 tokens per second speed but scores lower on intelligence (41) and lacks the 1000K context window, limiting its appeal for complex reasoning. The Hy3-preview model costs more at $0.10 per million tokens for intelligence of 42, resulting in a lower value score of 420, so it is only viable if specific architectural features are needed. Among cheaper options, Nova Micro at $0.03 offers high speed (299 t/s) but very low intelligence (10), useful only for trivial tasks.

For the most common cost-sensitive API use case—production inference where budget is the primary constraint—the actionable recommendation is to deploy MiMo-V2.5. It provides the highest intelligence-per-dollar ratio among all models evaluated, with a proven track record on coding and general reasoning tasks. If your workload is latency-sensitive and you can sacrifice a small amount of intelligence, substitute DeepSeek V4 Flash for its faster generation speed, while maintaining the same per-token cost and context length. Avoid paying a premium for marginal intelligence gains; the top three value-ranked models all sit at $0.06, making that price point the sweet spot for cost-effective quality.
